title: To establish new ZIP Codes for certain communities, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill tells the U.S. Postal Service that it must create new ZIP Codes for 14 communities in different states across the country. Each of these communities will get their own special ZIP Code that no other place uses. The Postal Service has 270 days to make these new ZIP Codes after the bill becomes law. The communities are located in states like California, Connecticut, Colorado, Florida, Iowa, Wisconsin, and Wyoming.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Establishing New ZIP Codes
sections.text: Within two hundred seventy days after this law is passed, the United States Postal Service must create a separate and unique ZIP Code for each of these eight communities. Each community will get its own ZIP Code that no other place uses. The communities are Eastvale in California, Scotland in Connecticut, Cooper City in Florida, Miami Lakes in Florida, Ocoee in Florida, Village of Estero in Florida, Urbandale in Iowa, and Mills in Wyoming.
